findings.message: Incompatible response shape: pact expects response body wrapped in {"content": [...]}, but provider endpoint GET /items returns list[ItemRead] which serializes directly to [...]. This is an uncompensated breaking change in response structure that will cause verification to fail.
findings.suggestion: Update the pact to match the actual provider response shape. The body should be [{"id": 1, "name": "alpha"}, {"id": 2, "name": "beta"}] instead of {"content": [{"id": 1, "name": "alpha"}]}, and update matching rules from "$[*]" to "$[*]" (applied to array items directly).

findings.message: Incomplete response expectation: pact only includes one item (id=1, name="alpha") in the response body, but the provider test seeds two items ("alpha" and "beta" at lines 37-38 of test_provider_pact.py). The actual /items endpoint will return both items, causing pact verification to fail due to response mismatch.
findings.suggestion: Add the second item to the pact response body or adjust the test provider state. The expected body should include both items: [{"id": 1, "name": "alpha"}, {"id": 2, "name": "beta"}] to match what the provider actually returns.

findings.message: Pact not regenerated after provider change: the committed pact (pacts/examplewebapp-app.json) does not match the current provider implementation. The provider's response shape and content differ from what the pact specifies, indicating the consumer-side pact generation needs to be re-run and published.
findings.suggestion: Run the consumer-driven contract test to regenerate the pact against the actual provider implementation, then commit and publish the updated pact to the Pact Broker.
